---
title: "Vensim Model Graph Example"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import re
import pandas as pd
from graphviz import Digraph
from IPython.display import SVG, display

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Path to the Vensim model
# ------------------------------------------------------------

MODEL_PATH = Path("data/DiamondDuckTheAlonsoMuthMills_GonerDuck.mdl")


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Minimal Vensim .mdl parser
# ------------------------------------------------------------

def clean_expression(expr: str) -> str:
    """Remove Vensim line continuations and extra whitespace."""
    expr = re.sub(r"\\\s*\n\s*", " ", expr)
    expr = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", expr)
    return expr.strip()


def classify_variable(expression: str) -> str:
    """Classify a Vensim variable based on its equation."""
    expr_upper = expression.upper()

    if "INTEG" in expr_upper:
        return "Stock"
    if "WITH LOOKUP" in expr_upper:
        return "Lookup"
    if re.fullmatch(r"[-+]?\d+(\.\d+)?([eE][-+]?\d+)?", expression.strip()):
        return "Constant"
    return "Auxiliary"


def parse_vensim_mdl(path: Path) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Parse variables, equations, units and comments from a Vensim .mdl file."""
    text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")

    # Remove Vensim sketch/layout section
    equation_text = text.split(r"\---/// Sketch information")[0]
    equation_text = equation_text.replace("{UTF-8}", "").strip()

    blocks = [block.strip() for block in equation_text.split("|") if block.strip()]
    rows = []

    for block in blocks:
        parts = block.split("\n\t~")

        equation_part = clean_expression(parts[0])
        units = parts[1].strip() if len(parts) > 1 else ""
        comment = parts[2].strip() if len(parts) > 2 else ""

        if "=" not in equation_part:
            continue

        name, expression = equation_part.split("=", 1)

        rows.append({
            "name": name.strip(),
            "type": classify_variable(expression.strip()),
            "equation": expression.strip(),
            "units": units,
            "comment": comment
        })

    return pd.DataFrame(rows)


model_df = parse_vensim_mdl(MODEL_PATH)

# Show all detected stocks
stocks = model_df[model_df["type"] == "Stock"][["name", "units", "equation"]]
stocks
```

```{python}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Extract dependencies between variables
# ------------------------------------------------------------

def extract_dependencies(model_df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Create edges: source variable -> target variable."""
    names = sorted(model_df["name"].tolist(), key=len, reverse=True)
    edges = []

    for _, row in model_df.iterrows():
        target = row["name"]
        expression = row["equation"]

        for source in names:
            if source == target:
                continue

            pattern = r"(?<![A-Za-z0-9_])" + re.escape(source) + r"(?![A-Za-z0-9_])"

            if re.search(pattern, expression):
                edges.append({
                    "source": source,
                    "target": target
                })

    return pd.DataFrame(edges).drop_duplicates()


edges_df = extract_dependencies(model_df)

edges_df.head()
```

```{python}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Build a small submodel around one stock
# ------------------------------------------------------------

FOCUS_STOCK = "Urban Land"
DEPTH = 2

def get_upstream_nodes(focus: str, edges_df: pd.DataFrame, depth: int = 2) -> set[str]:
    """
    Get all variables that influence the focus variable up to a given depth.
    This keeps the graph small and readable.
    """
    selected = {focus}
    frontier = {focus}

    for _ in range(depth):
        parents = set(edges_df[edges_df["target"].isin(frontier)]["source"])
        selected |= parents
        frontier = parents

    return selected


selected_nodes = get_upstream_nodes(FOCUS_STOCK, edges_df, depth=DEPTH)

sub_edges = edges_df[
    edges_df["source"].isin(selected_nodes)
    & edges_df["target"].isin(selected_nodes)
]

sub_nodes = model_df[model_df["name"].isin(selected_nodes)]


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Draw graph
# ------------------------------------------------------------

dot = Digraph("vensim_submodel", format="svg")
dot.attr(
    rankdir="LR",
    bgcolor="white",
    splines="ortho",
    nodesep="0.55",
    ranksep="0.9",
    fontname="Helvetica"
)

dot.attr(
    "node",
    shape="box",
    style="rounded,filled",
    fontname="Helvetica",
    fontsize="10",
    margin="0.12,0.08",
    color="#4b5563",
    fillcolor="#f9fafb"
)

dot.attr(
    "edge",
    color="#6b7280",
    arrowsize="0.7",
    penwidth="1.2",
    fontname="Helvetica",
    fontsize="9"
)

type_styles = {
    "Stock": {
        "shape": "box",
        "fillcolor": "#dbeafe",
        "color": "#1d4ed8",
        "penwidth": "2.0"
    },
    "Auxiliary": {
        "shape": "box",
        "fillcolor": "#f9fafb",
        "color": "#6b7280"
    },
    "Constant": {
        "shape": "ellipse",
        "fillcolor": "#ecfdf5",
        "color": "#047857"
    },
    "Lookup": {
        "shape": "component",
        "fillcolor": "#fef3c7",
        "color": "#b45309"
    }
}

for _, row in sub_nodes.iterrows():
    style = type_styles.get(row["type"], {})
    label = f"{row['name']}\n({row['type']})"
    dot.node(row["name"], label=label, **style)

for _, row in sub_edges.iterrows():
    dot.edge(row["source"], row["target"])

svg = dot.pipe().decode("utf-8")
display(SVG(svg))
```
